summary: Integration test suite for roadmap hardening stack: validates all five components work together without manual recovery

components.name: HeadSha Auto-Recovery
components.desc: Auto-detect and recover from git HEAD mismatch without manual intervention

tests.name: should detect HeadSha mismatch between git-state.json and current HEAD
should recover from HeadSha mismatch without manual intervention
should allow subsequent operations to proceed after HeadSha recovery

components.name: Preflight Validation Gates
components.desc: Pre-flight checks before roadmap operations: state coherence, artifact existence, typecheck

tests.name: should detect missing required artifacts in state
should block completion when artifacts are missing
should allow completion when all required artifacts exist
should validate artifact schema when specified
should prevent state advancement with unmet preflight checks

components.name: Trail Management & Auto-Commit
components.desc: Atomic trail.jsonl handling: auto-gitignore or auto-commit without manual friction

tests.name: should create trail.jsonl with structured entries
should append new trail entries without overwriting existing ones
should handle trail.jsonl with .gitignore to prevent conflicts
should auto-commit trail changes when auto-commit is enabled
should support manual trail mode (no auto-commit) by respecting .gitignore
should recover from trail corruption by validating JSONL format

components.name: DAG Switching & Validation
components.desc: Proper CLI for DAG switching (no symlinks): roadmap switch <dag-id>

tests.name: should list available DAGs from .roadmap/*.json files
should validate DAG consistency before switching
should not switch to invalid DAG
should preserve git state when switching DAGs
should orient correctly after switching DAGs

components.name: Artifact Validation Gates
components.desc: Validation gates: artifact existence, typecheck, schema compliance on completion

tests.name: should initialize roadmap with consistent git state
should detect mismatch, recover, and continue workflow
should enforce preflight checks throughout workflow
should track progress with immutable trail throughout workflow
should allow DAG switching without losing trail history
should complete full workflow: init → work → commit → trail → switch → verify
should handle HeadSha mismatch during workflow and auto-recover
should gate completion on all validation rules passing

scenarios.name: Scenario 1: HeadSha mismatch → auto-recovery → operation succeeds
scenarios.description: Detects when git HEAD diverges from recorded headSha in git-state.json, automatically recovers without manual intervention

scenarios.name: Scenario 2: Missing artifacts → preflight gate → completion blocked
scenarios.description: Validates that required artifacts exist before allowing node completion; blocks advancement when artifacts are missing or invalid

scenarios.name: Scenario 3: Trail changes → auto-commit → no git friction
scenarios.description: Manages trail.jsonl atomically: either auto-commits trail changes or ignores them via .gitignore, preventing merge conflicts and manual intervention

scenarios.name: Scenario 4: DAG switch → validates consistency → orients correctly
scenarios.description: Validates DAG structure before switching; validates all dependencies resolved, no cycles, init/term defined; re-orients correctly after switch

scenarios.name: Scenario 5: End-to-end: simulate real workflow with all components
scenarios.description: Full workflow integration: initialize → detect/recover mismatch → enforce preflight → track with trail → switch DAGs → verify all state remains consistent

acceptance_criteria.edge_cases_handled: ✓ Corruption, mismatch, and gap scenarios covered

notes: Integration tests are designed to run against the hardening-001 DAG when all component modules are available
Tests use isolated temporary git repos to avoid polluting the main repository
All 27 tests follow AAA pattern (Arrange → Act → Assert) for clarity
Each test is independent and can run in parallel
Trail corruption recovery validates JSONL line-by-line format
DAG validation checks for cycles, missing nodes, and missing init/term
Preflight checks include artifact existence, schema validation, and state coherence
All git operations are atomic (commit per logical unit)
No manual cleanup required — test repos are deleted after each test
